questions.question: Which of the following are considered positive symptoms of schizophrenia?

options.text: Difficulty with attention and working memory
Inability to experience pleasure and reduced speech

questions.explanation: Positive symptoms of schizophrenia represent additions to or distortions of normal experience and include hallucinations (perceptual experiences without external stimuli) and delusions (firmly held false beliefs). Flat affect and social withdrawal are negative symptoms.

questions.question: The dopamine hypothesis suggests that schizophrenia is associated with:

options.text: Deficiency of serotonin in the limbic system
Excessive dopamine activity in certain brain pathways
Overproduction of cortisol due to chronic stress

questions.explanation: The dopamine hypothesis proposes that schizophrenia is related to excessive dopamine activity in certain brain pathways. This is supported by the effectiveness of dopamine-blocking antipsychotic medications and the observation that dopamine-increasing drugs can produce psychotic symptoms.
